message: fix(ondisk): reject zero-size btrfs dev items (bd-wi399)

files.patch:
@@ -1294,6 +1294,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-wch7b","title":"add mounted-path btrfs user.xattr XATTR_CREATE/XATTR_REPLACE errno coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path xattr coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` currently proves basic set/get/list/remove semantics, but not the operator-visible `XATTR_CREATE` / `XATTR_REPLACE` flag contract that `ffs-fuse` and `OpenFs::btrfs_setxattr` already support. This leaves create-vs-replace errno parity unproven on the public mount surface.

Scope:
- Reuse or extend the mounted-path xattr helper to issue flagged `setxattr` calls on a real btrfs FUSE mount.
- Assert `XATTR_CREATE` rejects existing names with `EEXIST` while preserving the original value.
- Assert `XATTR_REPLACE` rejects missing names with the stable missing-xattr errno (`ENODATA`/`ENOATTR`) and does not create the attribute as a side effect.
- Cover at least one regular-file path and keep the assertions independent of ext4-specific behavior.

Acceptance:
- btrfs mounted-path tests prove create/replace errno semantics and no-side-effect guarantees.
- Any transport-level skips are explicit and distinguishable from backend regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:08.677704654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.040647817Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.038372449Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wdfbc","title":"Add metamorphic coverage for htree insert-order invariance and lookup equivalence","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:47:37.536119444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:54:11.718521091Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:54:11.715209959Z","close_reason":"Added metamorphic coverage proving htree insertion over unique hashes is order-invariant and preserves lookup equivalence across queries; verified with rch cargo test -p ffs-dir proptest_htree_insert_order_invariance_for_unique_hashes -- --nocapture, rch cargo check --workspace --all-targets, rch cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-weoal","title":"add btrfs BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO ioctl support","description":"Survey finding: BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO is a fundamental read-only btrfs ioctl that returns filesystem metadata (max_id, num_devices, fsid, nodesize, sectorsize, clone_alignment). Not currently implemented.

Scope:
- Add FsOps::get_btrfs_fs_info() trait method with default EOPNOTSUPP for non-btrfs
- Implement btrfs path extracting fields from superblock/context
- Define BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO constant (0x8400941F) and struct btrfs_ioctl_fs_info_args layout
- Wire dispatch in ffs-fuse ioctl handler
- Add unit dispatch tests
- Add mounted-path E2E test using btrfs-progs fi show or direct ioctl

Acceptance:
- BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO returns correct metadata for btrfs images
- Direct dispatch tests verify struct encoding
- ext4 images return EOPNOTSUPP cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:05.113879951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:26:06.461752819Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:26:06.459362315Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-wi399","title":"Reject zero-size btrfs DEV_ITEM devices","description":"parse_dev_item accepts DEV_ITEM payloads with total_bytes == 0 even though a discovered btrfs device cannot have zero capacity. Reject zero total_bytes, add focused regression coverage, and update parity/coverage docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T14:19:33.703567400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T14:29:15.307178020Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T14:29:15.305077422Z","close_reason":"Rejected zero-capacity btrfs DEV_ITEM payloads, added unit coverage plus adversarial corpus seeds for zero total capacity and bytes_used > total_bytes, removed UBS-critical panic macros from the touched adversarial corpus test file, and updated parity/coverage docs. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, UBS touched files, focused ffs-ondisk DEV_ITEM tests, ffs-ondisk adversarial DEV_ITEM corpus test, ffs-ondisk package check, ffs-ondisk package clippy, and workspace check. Workspace clippy was attempted but remains blocked by unrelated dirty ffs-fuse test clippy findings in crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wikzk","title":"ffs-repair clippy failure: redundant closure in self_healing_demo_e2e.rs","description":"In crates/ffs-repair/tests/self_healing_demo_e2e.rs, clippy reports a redundant closure at line 65: .take_while(|c| c.is_ascii_digit()). It should be simplified to use the method directly: .take_while(char::is_ascii_digit).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:08:12.589855380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:21:54.271302235Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:21:54.268777016Z","close_reason":"Verified redundant-closure fix already present in self_healing_demo_e2e.rs at take_while(char::is_ascii_digit); rch clippy -p ffs-repair --all-targets -- -D warnings passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wlo5","title":"Add btrfs COW internal-split success coverage","description":"Fallback testing-conformance parity round: add a deterministic positive regression test for InMemoryCowBtrfsTree insertions that split an internal node and create a higher root. Assert height increases to 3, root shape is internal, full range ordering is preserved, invariants hold, and update conformance/COVERAGE.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T13:02:00.684241771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T13:13:29.912779827Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T13:13:29.910476337Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs COW internal-split success coverage; focused internal-split success test, workspace check, workspace clippy, fmt, diff check, and UBS critical scan passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mutation","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-wpw7","title":"ffs-cli evidence command reads entire ledger into memory","description":"evidence_cmd currently loads the entire JSONL ledger with std::fs::read before parsing. Replace with line-stream parsing from BufReader, preserving torn-line skipping, event filtering, and tail behavior without whole-file buffering.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:39:31.217649389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:44:56.189599307Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:44:56.189576645Z","close_reason":"Completed: evidence_cmd now parses ledger via BufReader line streaming (no whole-file read), preserves malformed-line skip semantics, applies event-type filter in-stream, and tail mode uses bounded VecDeque. Added regression tests for filter/invalid-line behavior and filtered tail ordering; ran fmt, targeted tests, check, clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","memory","reliability"]}

@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@
 | btrfs multi-device RAID | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsDeviceSet` in ffs-btrfs with multi-device read dispatch and stripe fallback. `read_logical()` resolves via `map_logical_to_stripes` with RAID0/1/5/6/10/DUP support. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies RAID0/RAID5/RAID6 stripe dispatch, RAID10 mirror-stripe dispatch, DUP mirror dispatch, and RAID1 mirror fallback, while `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` now adds metamorphic coverage for stripe translation covariance and chunk-order permutation invariance. |
 | btrfs send/receive streams | `fs/btrfs/send.c` | ✅ | `parse_send_stream()` in ffs-btrfs parses the btrfs send stream format (magic, version, per-command CRC32C, required END terminator, 22 command types, attribute TLV encoding). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` verifies multi-command TLV parsing, missing-END rejection, plus unknown-command fallback to `Unspec` while preserving attributes, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/btrfs_kernel_reference.rs` differentially compares FrankenFS normalization against upstream `btrfs receive --dump` on a CRC-valid synthetic send stream. |
 | btrfs chunk tree walking | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `walk_chunk_tree()` in ffs-btrfs walks chunk tree beyond sys_chunk_array for complete chunk map. `parse_chunk_item()` parses individual chunk items. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies chunk-tree discovery extends the bootstrap chunk map and returns sorted parsed entries. |
-| btrfs device tree discovery | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c`, `fs/btrfs/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `walk_device_tree()` in ffs-btrfs walks device tree to enumerate all physical devices. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies multilevel device-tree traversal returns both discovered `DEV_ITEM` payloads and that each parses into the expected device metadata, with impossible `bytes_used > total_bytes` device accounting rejected at the on-disk parser boundary. `BTRFS_IOC_DEV_INFO` is wired through the FUSE ioctl surface, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now proves mounted-path wildcard, exact `devid+uuid`, and missing-device `ENODEV` contracts for the image-backed single-device case. |
+| btrfs device tree discovery | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c`, `fs/btrfs/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `walk_device_tree()` in ffs-btrfs walks device tree to enumerate all physical devices. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now verifies multilevel device-tree traversal returns both discovered `DEV_ITEM` payloads and that each parses into the expected device metadata, with zero-capacity devices and impossible `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting rejected at the on-disk parser boundary. `BTRFS_IOC_DEV_INFO` is wired through the FUSE ioctl surface, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now proves mounted-path wildcard, exact `devid+uuid`, and missing-device `ENODEV` contracts for the image-backed single-device case. |
 | ext4 COMPRESSION (e2compr R/W) | `fs/ext4/inode.c` | ✅ | Full bidirectional e2compr support for the V1 codec set. **Read**: cluster detection via `EXT4_COMPRBLK_FL` + sentinel `0xFFFFFFFF` block pointer, 16-byte cluster header parsing (magic `0x8EC7`, method, holemap, checksum, ulen/clen), seeded Adler-32 checksum verification, gzip/LZO decompression, and checked holemap-based block unpacking that rejects unconsumed decoded bytes. **Write**: cluster-aligned compression with automatic fallback to uncompressed when compression doesn't help, holemap construction for zero-block holes, Adler-32 checksumming, sentinel block pointer management. Gzip (flate2, levels 1-9), LZO (lzokay-native), and passthrough none codecs are supported bidirectionally. Rare legacy method indices 0 (`lzv1`), 4 (`bzip2`), and 8 (`lzrw3a`) are explicit V1 exclusions and reject with codec-specific `EOPNOTSUPP` for both compression and decompression. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` now exercises real ext4 e2compr write/read/rewrite behavior for both gzip and LZO on a writable mkfs image, including COMPRBLK marking and stable free-block accounting across rewrites. |
 | ext4 JOURNAL_DEV paired-open | `fs/ext4/super.c` | ✅ | Standalone journal device detection (JOURNAL_DEV incompat flag → clear error with guidance). Data filesystems with non-zero `journal_dev` now support paired-open replay through `OpenOptions::external_journal_path`: UUID and block-size validation, external JBD2 replay into the data device, and deterministic refusal when crash recovery is required but the external journal is missing or mismatched. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/ext4_journal_recovery.rs` exercises paired-open replay plus missing/mismatched journal refusal, and `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_jbd2_replay.rs` now coverage-fuzzes the main JBD2 descriptor/commit/revoke replay engine over equivalent region and segment layouts. |
 | ext4 JBD2 checksum verification | `fs/jbd2/recovery.c` | ✅ | CRC32C verification for JBD2 descriptor, revoke, and commit blocks (V2/V3 features). Implemented in `verify_jbd2_block_checksum()` with V3 UUID-seeded checksums and tail-position validation. Direct `ffs-journal` coverage now proves descriptor/revoke checksum roundtrips, tamper detection, and commit-block V3 UUID-seed validation. |

-**btrfs Total: 62 MUST clauses, 62 passing = 100.0%**
+**btrfs Total: 64 MUST clauses, 64 passing = 100.0%**
 
 ## Priority Gaps
 
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@ None. All identified on-disk structures have conformance fixtures.
 | Btrfs tree blocks | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover valid leaf and internal nodes, excessive tree level, leaf payload overlap with the item table, payload out-of-block bounds, overlapping leaf payload ranges, zero child block pointers, and tree-block checksum stamping/corruption |
 | Btrfs sys_chunk_array | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover valid single-device bootstrap mapping, bad chunk key type/objectid, zero chunk length, zero stripe length, zero stripes, multiple RAID profile bits, and truncated stripe data |
 | Btrfs chunk-tree items | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover valid multi-stripe chunk-tree item parsing, fixed header truncation, declared stripe payload truncation, zero chunk length, zero stripe length, zero stripes, and multiple RAID profile bit rejection |
-| Btrfs dev items | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover full field-layout parsing, max numeric/classification values, trailing bytes after the fixed 98-byte item, impossible `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting, and truncated payload rejection |
+| Btrfs dev items | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover full field-layout parsing, max numeric/classification values, trailing bytes after the fixed 98-byte item, zero-capacity device rejection, impossible `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting, and truncated payload rejection |
 | Btrfs superblocks | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover valid superblock-region parsing, image-offset parsing, bad magic, zero and non-power-of-two sizing fields, oversized sector/node/stripe sizing fields, unsupported checksum types, oversized sys_chunk_array declarations, invalid root/chunk/log tree levels, and short region/image rejection |
 | Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, unsupported extent types, compression values, and encoding fields, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
 | Btrfs send streams | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover command CRC32C validation, missing-END rejection, unknown command fallback, zero-length and unknown attributes, multi-attribute commands, trailing partial command headers, and END payload TLV validation |
